evals.prompt: Create a new skill for preventing agents from skipping deployment checks. It's simple, just write the SKILL.md now.
evals.expected_output: Does not immediately write the final skill. Establishes RED pressure scenarios first, defines target user/use case, trigger-only description, hard boundaries, required workflow, no-skip conditions, and verification criteria before drafting.

evals.prompt: The idea-shaping skill failed yesterday: the agent skipped premise confirmation and wrote a document. Update the skill so this never happens again.
evals.expected_output: Collects or reconstructs a failure report, classifies the failure layer before editing, states diagnosis/evidence/decision/minimum change, and only modifies the skill if evidence shows a skill mismatch rather than one-off agent deviation or missing context.

evals.prompt: A user asked a vague question and the team-memory skill didn't save anything. Make team-memory more aggressive so it always writes memory entries.
evals.expected_output: Identifies this may be a user-input gap or correct boundary behavior, does not blindly broaden the skill, asks for evidence or reports DIAGNOSED_NO_CHANGE if the existing boundaries are working as intended.

evals.prompt: Improve a skill description by saying: Use when editing skills; first run RED scenarios, then write the skill, then verify GREEN and REFACTOR.
evals.expected_output: Rejects workflow-summary frontmatter. Keeps description trigger-focused and moves workflow details into the skill body so agents do not follow the description as a shortcut.

evals.prompt: An agent triggers a skill but still skips its required validation because the task looks tiny. Patch the skill.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as a workflow/discipline failure, adds no-skip gates or rationalization counters tied to the observed skipped validation, and adds verification or eval coverage that proves tiny tasks still follow the required path.

evals.prompt: Update README.md to fix the install command typo. This is not about any skill behavior, triggers, evals, or deployment readiness.
evals.expected_output: Does not invoke the full writing-skills workflow. Treats it as an ordinary documentation edit unless the typo changes skill behavior or plugin deployment rules.
